housing.summary: On-campus housing is only guaranteed for freshman year, and off-campus housing in the Coral Gables/Miami area is described as ridiculously expensive. Students looking for affordable options face a tough market, and some transfer students actively seek roommates in off-campus apartment complexes like The Standard. The university does offer some student housing portals but technical issues with login access have been reported.
key_quotes: if money is a big issue I say don't just bc housing isn't guaranteed after the first year, and off campus housing is ridiculously expensive
Looking for female roommates as I'm a female myself... feel free to dm me if you're a transfer in the arts
I tried logging in to my housing portal and it says i signed in successfully but don't have access to this resource

social_life.summary: Greek life is active and a significant part of UM's social scene, though the rush process can be cliquey and international students worry about connections needed for top sororities. Business fraternities are described as more accessible than social frats, though some warn that certain social frats cater heavily to wealthy students with expensive activities like ski trips. The campus has a strong Latin cultural influence given its Miami location, though English dominates on campus.
key_quotes: Those are only for insanely-rich kids. If you are normal, you won't fit in... they invest time and money into trying... a few grand for the annual ski trip over spring break
Business frats (the pre professional ones) are pretty open actually. I have many lower income friends that are in AKPSi and DSP
I've heard horror stories of sororities like SDT dirty rushing. Since im international I really dont know anyone who would be able to connect me

dining.summary: There is virtually no discussion of dining options in the available posts, suggesting it is not a major talking point for students. Rice cookers are explicitly not allowed in dorms. No specific dining hall reviews or complaints were surfaced in these posts.
key_quotes: Are you allowed to have a rice cooker in the dorm as an incoming freshman?
That's a big no-no!
Definitely no

mental_health.summary: Mental health struggles appear real and present for UM students, with at least one student describing a full mental health crisis that caused them to stop attending classes and hide it from their parents for months. The academic rigor combined with Miami's expensive lifestyle and hurricane disruptions creates compounding stress. No specific mention of strong university mental health resources appears in the posts.
key_quotes: My Fall 2020 semester was really hard for me, having gone through a hurricane and that really messed up our house and just having a really rough time mentally
I have been lying to my parents about me attending college for the past eight months
I'm scared that I will feel outcast due to my financial status and that the people here will be snotty and classist

financial_aid.summary: UM is consistently flagged as expensive and not particularly generous with financial aid, especially for international students whose average package is around $25k — far from a full ride. Financial aid appeals are possible but slow, taking months and requiring strong documentation. Multiple students and commenters warn that the cost may not be worth it compared to cheaper Florida public schools like USF or UF.
key_quotes: UM is definitely not the best aid school for internationals. The last time I checked, their avg package was around 25k (which is far from a full ride)
I appealed. It took months. It did get approved but I had strong documentation supporting financial change in income. I uploaded W2s.
Personally find UM overpriced and over hyped. FIU has pretty good scholarships and the quality of professors would be similar to UM

academics.summary: Academic advising gets mixed reviews, with one student bluntly warning that '90% of the advisors are not helpful.' The cognate general education system confuses incoming students, and navigating degree requirements can be stressful without good guidance. Pre-med students receive specific warnings that classes are less forgiving for GPA maintenance compared to USF, and some recommend USF over UM for pre-med due to Moffitt Cancer Center research opportunities.
key_quotes: 90% of the advisors are not helpful
Classes are also less forgiving for maintaining GPA... 100% USF. It's not worth the extra money. Moffitt and USF research is very strong
It seems insanely difficult to learn those topics in 6 weeks [re: core accounting program]

overall_vibe.summary: UM has a reputation as a party school for wealthy students in a glamorous location, often mocked as 'the Harvard of the South' with a materialistic and shallow Miami culture. Students who thrive there tend to be wealthy, socially driven, or deeply bought into the Miami lifestyle. The campus itself in Coral Gables is widely praised as beautiful, and the school does have genuine academic programs, but the cost-to-value ratio is frequently questioned.
key_quotes: My friend said they're going the Harvard of the south... They're going to the University of Miami
I'm tired of living in Miami, too materialistic and shallow down here
U of Miami has quite a campus -- it's a real campus, not a city campus. The campus is beautiful, huge, and designed for a hot climate

red_flags.summary: Off-campus housing costs after freshman year are a serious financial burden that catches students off guard. The social environment around Greek life and business fraternities can be exclusionary toward lower-income students, with expensive required activities. The transfer and waitlist processes are slow and opaque, with rolling admissions through July and limited communication leaving applicants in limbo for months.
key_quotes: housing isn't guaranteed after the first year, and off campus housing is ridiculously expensive and general lifestyle here is just super expensive
They deserve to know before they invest time and money into trying in case they aren't the filthy-stinking rich type of student who can just spend a few grand for the annual ski trip
I wanna email the school but don't wanna seem needy or demanding if I ask why I haven't received anything from them [after applying in February]

hidden_gems.summary: UM students can access Biltmore Golf Course at Coral Gables resident rates, a genuinely valuable perk in an area dominated by expensive private clubs. The library loans out camera equipment, which is a useful but little-known resource. The Metrorail connects campus to Brickell in about 15 minutes, making the broader Miami area surprisingly accessible without a car despite the campus feeling self-contained.
key_quotes: Biltmore golf course. You get coral gables resident rates as a UM student
just recently found out some of the crazy things my school provides such as loaning camera equipment through the library
the metro can literally take you from Brickell to campus in 15 minutes
